title: No alert grouping — single incident generates 50 notifications
code_snippet:
# Alertmanager config
route:
  receiver: pagerduty-team
  group_by: ['...']  # effectively no grouping
  group_wait: 0s
  group_interval: 0s
# When DB goes down, each of 50 service instances fires HighErrorRate
# Result: 50 separate PagerDuty incidents
expected_feedback: Standard: Alertmanager has no effective grouping — group_by is wildcard and group_wait is 0. When the database goes down, all 50 service instances fire HighErrorRate simultaneously, creating 50 separate PagerDuty incidents for the same root cause. On-call receives 50 pages in rapid succession, making it harder to focus on the actual problem. Fix: group_by: ['alertname', 'severity'] to batch same-alert instances into one notification. Set group_wait: 30s to collect related alerts before sending. Add inhibition rules: if DatabaseDown is firing, suppress HighErrorRate from dependent services.
